headline: BREAKING: Iran formally closes Strait of Hormuz, threatens to fire on ships — Trump then signals deal 'essentially agreed,' cancels further strikes. S&P +1.8%.
sub: Jun 11: The Strait of Hormuz has been formally declared closed by Iran's joint military command — any vessel attempting passage will be shot at. Hours later, Trump canceled further strikes and said 'all points of a deal are essentially agreed upon,' sending equities sharply higher (S&P +1.8%, Nasdaq +2.5%, Dow +930pts). The situation remains fluid: a formal closure of the world's most critical oil chokepoint is a new threshold even as ceasefire diplomacy accelerates. US strikes hit Qeshm, Bandar Abbas, Minab, and Sirik in the early hours of Jun 11. PPI came in hot — fastest pace in nearly four years — adding inflation pressure just as Warsh prepares for his June 17 debut presser.

events.text: BREAKING — Iran Formally Closes Strait of Hormuz; Threatens Ships (Jun 11)
events.detail: Iran's joint military command declared the full closure of the Strait of Hormuz on Jun 11, including for oil tankers and all commercial shipping. Any vessel attempting passage will be fired upon. This is a new threshold beyond disruption risk — a formal closure of the world's most critical oil chokepoint. Roughly 20% of global oil supply transits the Strait daily. If enforced, crude could spike to $120+ and global energy markets face a structural supply shock.

events.text: BREAKING — Trump Signals Peace Deal 'Essentially Agreed'; Cancels Further Strikes (Jun 11)
events.detail: President Trump announced the US would cancel further strikes on Iran and said 'all points of a deal are essentially agreed upon.' S&P 500 surged 1.8%, Nasdaq +2.5%, Dow +930 points on the news. Qatari mediators remain active. The situation is fast-moving — Hormuz closure declaration and deal signal occurred within hours of each other on Jun 11. Do not assume the rally is sustainable until a formal ceasefire and Hormuz reopening are confirmed.

events.text: BREAKING — US Launches Fresh Strikes Jun 11 (Qeshm, Bandar Abbas, Minab, Sirik)
events.detail: US strikes continued into Jun 11, hitting Qeshm, Bandar Abbas, Minab, and Sirik in the hours after midnight Tehran time. Defense Secretary Hegseth stated 'CENTCOM will be busy tonight.' This preceded Trump's peace signal later in the day — the conflict's trajectory in the next 24-48 hours is the dominant market variable.

events.text: BREAKING — Iran Strikes US Bases: Jordan, Bahrain & Kuwait (Jun 10)
events.detail: Iran's Revolutionary Guard launched missiles and drones at US military bases in Jordan, Bahrain, and Kuwait on Jun 10 in direct retaliation for US strikes near the Strait of Hormuz. 'Just about all' projectiles intercepted. Trump threatened to hit Iran 'very hard' if no peace deal. This was the first direct Iranian attack on US military installations across multiple allied nations.

events.text: BREAKING — US Strikes Iran (Strait of Hormuz, Jun 9)
events.detail: US launched retaliatory strikes Jun 9 after Iran downed a US Apache helicopter off Oman. Explosions at Bandar Abbas and Qeshm — directly on the Strait of Hormuz. Ceasefire collapsed. Crude oil spike and shipping disruption risk are acute.

events.text: May PPI (Thu Jun 11) — RESULT: Hottest in Nearly 4 Years
events.detail: Producer prices surged at the fastest pace since late 2022 — the energy-shock pipeline is showing up clearly. Combined with May CPI at 4.2%, this raises the stakes materially for Warsh's June 17 debut presser. A hot PPI gives hawks ammunition even as Iran deal hopes provide short-term risk-on relief.

events.text: May CPI (Wed Jun 10) — RESULT: 4.2% YoY, core +0.2% MoM
events.detail: 4.2% YoY came in-line with expectations; core monthly 0.2% softer than feared. Not a disaster but near two-year highs. PPI on Jun 11 came in even hotter, reinforcing pipeline inflation pressure heading into June 17.
First major central bank move since Iran ceasefire; guidance sets EUR/USD tone and signals global policy direction — now complicated by the Hormuz formal closure and rapid deal signals.

events.text: Fed Rate Decision & Warsh Debut Presser (Tue Jun 17)
events.detail: Warsh's first press conference as Fed chair is the next major macro pivot point. Hot PPI + 4.2% CPI vs. potential Hormuz de-escalation creates a genuinely difficult policy landscape. Rate-hike odds at 57% post-payrolls — watch for Warsh's tone on energy-driven inflation.

Household inflation expectations running hotter than forecaster models. Iran base strikes and Hormuz closure will weigh on confidence — but peace deal signal may partially offset. Watch the inflation expectations component closely.

bias: Fast-moving — do not position hard in either direction until Hormuz status is confirmed. Iran formally closing the Strait of Hormuz is the highest-impact oil-supply event in decades, but Trump's same-day peace signal complicates the picture sharply. The Jun 11 equity rally (+1.8% S&P) prices in deal success — if the Hormuz closure is not formally lifted within 24-48 hours, that rally reverses hard. Hot PPI (fastest in nearly 4 years) + 4.2% CPI means Warsh is walking into his June 17 debut in a stagflation trap. Energy (XLE, OXY) and gold remain the structural hedges. Do not chase the equity rally until a signed ceasefire and confirmed Hormuz reopening. USD strength likely persists regardless of outcome.
